channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Chris Brown pleads guilty to affray after London nightclub altercation as more serious charges are withdrawn
item.description: Grammy Award-winning singer Chris Brown pleaded guilty to a charge of affray during a hearing at London's Southwark Crown Court, bringing a significant development in a criminal case linked to an inci...

item.title: President Marcos completes nationwide distribution of patient transport vehicles
item.description: President Ferdinand 'Bongbong' Marcos Jr. announced the full distribution of Patient Transport Vehicles (PTVs) to all 82 provinces, cities, and municipalities in the Philippines. The initiative aims t...

item.title: Gambia's role in shaping colonial trade and anti-slavery efforts
item.description: The article explores the overlooked historical significance of The Gambia, highlighting how its river basin played a key role in colonial trade and the fight against slavery. Pape Chérif Bertrand Bass...

item.title: Digital Services and Network Expansion Boost Africa's Telecom Growth
item.description: The article discusses how expanding digital services and improving telecommunications networks are key to driving growth in Africa's telecom sector. It highlights efforts by governments and private co...

item.title: Roughriders Suffer Heartbreaking Loss to Elks in Emotional Game
item.description: The Regina Roughriders experienced a devastating defeat against the Edmonton Elks in an emotionally charged matchup that left fans heartbroken. The game, which took place on July 23, 2026, saw the Rou...

item.title: Railway Expansion Project Boosts Connectivity and Freight in Karnataka and Andhra
item.description: The Cabinet Committee on Economic Affairs (CCEA) has approved a ₹1,264 crore railway line expansion project in Karnataka and Andhra Pradesh, spanning 46 km across three districts. This multi-tracking ...

item.title: Tanzania supports rural business growth through fuel station funding
item.description: A group of rural entrepreneurs in Tanzania has secured financial assistance from institutions like TDFI and CDB to establish fuel stations across remote regions. This initiative aims to boost local ec...

item.title: Abuja realtor faces charges over alleged N500m land fraud and money laundering
item.description: The Economic and Financial Crimes Commission (EFCC) has arraigned a prominent Abuja realtor, Bilikisu Ishaku Aliyu, on charges of orchestrating a N500m land fraud scheme. The case involves forged docu...

item.title: Protesters Escape Police Van During Mumbai Demonstration
item.description: A viral video captured a youth secretly opening the rear door of a police detention van during a demonstration in Mumbai's Chembur area. The footage shows detained Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) proteste...

item.title: Central Government Appoints Social Leaders Against Congress Party Members
item.description: The central government has appointed social leaders to counter members of the Congress party who were withdrawn from the party. This decision comes amid growing political tensions between the ruling p...

item.title: Canada and Ecuador finalize free trade agreement in Ottawa
item.description: The Canadian and Ecuadorian governments are finalizing a free trade agreement in Ottawa, marking a significant step in bilateral economic relations. The deal, which removes tariffs on all Canadian imp...

item.title: Trump's Iran Policy Sparks Gas Price Surge and Political Backlash
item.description: President Donald Trump's escalating conflict with Iran has led to a significant surge in gasoline prices, raising concerns about its political ramifications. According to recent data, gas prices jumpe...

item.title: ICC member states vote on disciplinary action against prosecutor Karim Khan
item.description: The International Criminal Court's (ICC) 125 member states are set to determine the future of prosecutor Karim Khan, who faces allegations of sexual assault against a female staff member. The decision...

item.title: R&B singer Chris Brown admits guilt in Mayfair nightclub altercation
item.description: R&B singer Chris Brown has pleaded guilty to affray following a confrontation at a Mayfair club in 2023. The 37-year-old, who had initially denied charges of assault and weapon possession, now faces s...

item.title: Betty Pack: The Daring WWII Spy Who Used Seduction to Aid the Allies
item.description: Betty Pack, codenamed 'Cynthia,' was a pivotal American-born spy for British MI6 and U.S. OSS during World War II. Her intelligence work included infiltrating Axis networks, contributing to the decryp...

item.title: DepEd expands mental health support in public schools with 10,000 new counselor positions
item.description: The Department of Education (DepEd) has initiated the nationwide recruitment of 10,000 School Counselor Associate I positions to enhance mental health services in public schools. This move follows the...

item.title: GOP Operatives Doubt Trump's Endorsement Will Secure Darline Graham Nordone's Senate Race
item.description: U.S. Senator Darline Graham Nordone (R-SC), sister of the late Sen. Lindsey Graham, faces skepticism from Republican operatives despite President Donald Trump's endorsement in South Carolina's Senate ...

item.title: CJP and government meet for 2 hours on exam irregularities and minister resignation demands
item.description: The 'Cockroach Janta Party' (CJP) held a two-hour meeting with Union ministers JP Nadda and Jitendra Singh amid ongoing protests at Jantar Mantar over alleged exam irregularities, particularly the NEE...

item.title: Modi's Australia Visit and the Indian Diaspora's Mixed Reactions
item.description: The article discusses Prime Minister Narendra Modi's visit to Australia, where he addressed a large Indian-Australian crowd in Melbourne. The piece contrasts this positive reception with recent protes...
